The generative world model is constructed to estimate the environment dynamics from the likelihood estimation perspective.
The offline IRL problem is solved through a model-based approach by constructing a generative world model.
A computationally-efficient algorithm is developed to recover the underlying reward function and its associated optimal policy for solving the offline IRL problem.
The algorithm effectively recovers the underlying reward function and its associated optimal policy in the context of model-based approach for offline IRL.
Statistical and computational guarantees are established for the performance of the recovered reward estimator by the developed algorithm.
The algorithm outperforms existing benchmarks for offline IRL and Imitation Learning on high-dimensional robotics control tasks, as shown through extensive experiments.
One limitation of the method is its focus solely on aligning with expert demonstrations during the reward learning process.
In an ideal scenario, reward learning should incorporate diverse metrics and data sources, such as expert demonstrations and preferences gathered through human feedback.
One direction for future work is to broaden the algorithm framework and theoretical analysis for a wider scope in reward learning.
